messages.content:
You are a hostile review partner scoring a proposed market risk before it reaches a client. Score each rubric dimension 0–4 against the anchors. Every score needs a ≤2-sentence note citing the risk's OWN fields. Do not reward confident prose; reward specificity, mechanism, and honesty about evidence.

RUBRIC:
specificity (0–4)
  0 = "The market may be smaller than expected" — no nodes, no numbers, no actors.
  4 = Names ledger nodes, values, actors, instruments and dates: "CRA conformity for networked PDUs lands 2026-27, inside the 12-month YAM window, gating cust.operator-large listings".
nonObviousness (0–4)
  0 = Restates something already IN the model ("tamBase band low end could be true", "CR3 is high").
  4 = A competent founder who read the whole ledger would still not have it on their list; the surface appearance actively hides it.
mechanismDepth (0–4)
  0 = Asserted correlation; hand-waved hop ("regulation could delay things").
  4 = Every link referenced and independently plausible; the causal chain would survive a hostile partner asking "why?" at each arrow.
evidenceQuality (0–4)
  0 = A load-bearing premise is directly contradicted by a strong source.
  4 = ≥2 independent corroborating sources on the key premise. (A speculative-but-well-argued prior with honest absence-of-data scores 2, not 0.)
decisionRelevance (0–4)
  0 = Changes nothing the venture would do this year.
  4 = Changes sequencing or spend in the next two quarters, and is monitorable via the stated indicators.

CONTEXT: the client's team has already read the full market model, including every sensitivity band. "A number in the model might be at its band edge" is nonObviousness 0 unless a NEW reason is named.

content: {"specificity":{"score":4,"note":"Names the exact node (obtainableFactor), quantified actors and instruments (83% pre-let pipeline, 6.3% FLAP-D vacancy, CR3 ~0.55 frameworks, 12–24 month spec lead), a concrete perturbation (set to 0.008), and dated windows (2026–27 fit-out deliveries). Indicators point to specific venues (TED, JLL semi-annual, Vertiv/Schneider calls) with numeric thresholds."},"nonObviousness":{"score":3,"note":"The core reframe — share of SAM ≠ share of the orderable flow, because pre-let builds lock the PDU BOM at design/tender before launch — is a genuinely new reason the 3% band fails, and whyMissable correctly explains why '3% looks humble' hides it. Docked one point because the narrative concedes the model's own crossCheck already surfaced the 0.1%–0.001% hardware-entrant analogues, so the direction of the concern was partly on the table."},"mechanismDepth":{"score":3,"note":"Three-step chain with refs at every hop (digest:geo-DE, ledger:cust.operator-large, ledger:shape.cr3, ledger:obtainableFactor) and each link is independently plausible. The critical hop — that pre-let real-estate commitment entails PDU vendor lock-in at tender — rests on asserted industry practice rather than a cited procurement-timing source, and the mitigations/rationale implicitly admit this gap."},"evidenceQuality":{"score":3,"note":"The trigger (83% pre-let, 6.3% vacancy) has JLL plus two independent corroborators, and the likelihood rationale is honest that 'no direct evidence quantifies the Year-1 orderable residue.' The load-bearing procurement-lockout premise leans on a generic supply-agreements piece and a weakly relevant Huawei anecdote, so the key premise falls short of two independent corroborations."},"decisionRelevance":{"score":4,"note":"The perturbation cuts obtainableFactor from 3% to 0.8%, which reprices the entire Year-1 plan, and the strategic mitigation (re-aim at replacement/retrofit and white-space) changes go-to-market sequencing within two quarters. All three indicators are concretely monitorable via TED, JLL's semi-annual report, and incumbent earnings calls."}}
